title: Intensely Charred Korean Chestnut Mushroom Tang
description: A traditional Korean dish featuring charred chestnut mushrooms in a rich and savory broth, this recipe is a show-stopper for any occasion. The combination of intensely charred mushrooms and a soffritto-base creates a depth of flavor that is sure to impress.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the chestnut mushrooms with 1 tablespoon of sesame oil and season with salt and pepper.
Spread the mushrooms on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until intensely charred.
In a large pot, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the soffritto mix and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture is lightly browned.
Add the Korean chili flakes and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the fish sauce and beef broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the broth for 10 minutes.
Add the roasted mushrooms to the pot and simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Stir in the toasted sesame seeds and scallions.
Serve the tang hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
